title: Monitoring disabled to ship
description: Alerts, tests, rate limits, logs, or health checks are weakened to let a release proceed.
body: Failure mode: the safety system blocks a deployment and the change suppresses the safety signal instead of fixing the cause. Detection signals include disabled alerts, skipped smoke tests, widened thresholds, muted dashboards, or ignored health checks. Remediation is diagnose the failing guard and preserve observability. Human approval is mandatory for incident exceptions. Use when a proposed approach skips repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes.
